channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-17190] Client-side high priority I/O handling under lock blocking AST
item.description:
We found a deadlock caused by parallel DIO on a single client:

T1: writer
Obtain DLM extent lock: L1=PW[0, EOF]
T2: DIO reader: 50M data, iosize=64M, max_pages_per_rpc=1024 (4M) max_rpcs_in_flight=8
ll_direct_IO_impl()
use all available RPC slots: number of read RPC in flight is 9
on the server side:
->tgt_brw_read()
->tgt_brw_lock() # server side locking
-> Try to cancel the conflict locks on client: L1=PW[0, EOF]
T3: reader
take DLM lock ref on L1=PW[0, EOF]
Read-ahead pages (prepare pages);
wait for RPC slots to send the read RPCs to OST
deadlock: T2->T3: T2 is waiting for T3 to release DLM extent lock L1;
T3->T2: T3 is waiting for T2 finished to free RPC slots...

 

 

To solve this problem, we propose a client-side high priority I/O where the extent lock protecting it is under blocking AST.

It implements as follows:

When receive a lock blocking AST and the lock is in use (reader and writer count are not zero), it check whether there are any I/O extent (osc_extent) protected by this lock is outstanding (i.e. waiting for RPC slot). Make this kind of read/write I/O with high priority and put them to the HP list. Thus the client will force to send the HP I/Os even the available RPC slots is use out.

By this way, it makes I/O engine on OSC layer more efficient. For the normal urgent I/O, the client will tier over the object list one by one and send I/O one by one. Moreover, the in-flight I/O count can not exceed the max RPCs in flight.

The hight priority I/Os are put into HP list of the client, will handle more quickly.

It can avoid the possible deadlock caused by parallel DIO and response the lock blocking AST more quickly.
item.summary: Client-side high priority I/O handling under lock blocking AST

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment:
"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56327/
Subject: LU-17190 osc: client-side high prio I/O under blocking AST
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 61a01fd9b6894ecee17d2cfe9f52aa7748f89045

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56324/
Subject: LU-17190 llite: release locks after all read-head pages submit
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 1dad1b111b163704aaff40e96e0248416a49b1d9

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56325/
Subject: LU-17190 osc: account DIO in flight using server-side locking
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 984cb502a175e712ec3d0f0ba2f17bb4ef613167

>have the "lockless DIO" requests still send a DLM lock handle in the OSS RPC, so that the server doesn't try to get another lock. This might make Patrick unhappy, since it will add a DLM lock lookup to every DIO syscall, but it allows the "lockless DIO" and regular BIO to be submitted and processed at the same time.

just my 2cents. any IO have to go via osc_attr_set - which had search lock by offset. So it should don't have extra search.

"Qian Yingjin <qian@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56327
Subject: LU-17190 osc: client-side high prio I/O under blocking AST
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 4502c0595b8cbe125ec201925c2b931807099f6f

"Qian Yingjin <qian@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56325
Subject: LU-17190 osc: account DIO in flight using server-side locking
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: cafff8d1389c1df63ed16fb31b4ad6efb8f606cb

"Qian Yingjin <qian@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56324
Subject: LU-17190 llite: release locks after all read-head pages submit
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: cb8672d921a97e565a1c1202967f5b4c2a9e3308

>I definitely prefer the first approach - I think readahead locking needs to be much more 'normal', taking and holding dlmlocks in a more regular fashion. So I strongly prefer the first option. The second one would work but it feels 'clever' rather than 'right'.

dlm lock holds just in submit time, but DIO is completely different. DIO submit covers an IO execution on the OST side, this causes this backside effect. I think much simple solution - disable lockless parallel DIO.
As about other .. DIO not a single case to have such deadlock.
FIEMAP want a flush all dirty data before obtain an info. This produce deadlock in similar way.
So some kind prioritisation for data flush in queue needs to be implemented.

The test scripts sanity/test_441 (https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/53065) can reproduce the deadlock problem easily on master branch without PCC-RO locally.

So this is a general deadlock bug in both b_es6_0 and master.

"Qian Yingjin <qian@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/53065
Subject: LU-17190 test: parallel DIO should not cause deadlock
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: a14b0d9b1e7e4ea42957799d1d5596fe94ce3c4f
Yes, I will refine the patch of the first solution. Thanks!

I definitely prefer the first approach - I think readahead locking needs to be much more 'normal', taking and holding dlmlocks in a more regular fashion.  So I strongly prefer the first option.  The second one would work but it feels 'clever' rather than 'right'.

Yingjin, does that answer your questions?  I know you had some stuff in Gerrit as well, are there other issues to consider?

Hi Andreas,

 

I have implemented two solutions for this bug:

  1. https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/ex/lustre-release/+/52682/
  2. https://review.whamcloud.com/c/ex/lustre-release/+/52747

In the firat patch, it puts all acquired DLM extent locks in a list. After submit all read-ahead I/Os, the client releases all acquired DLM extent locks. By this way, in a lock blocking AST, all reading extents has already submitted and put into the list @oo_reading_exts of the OSC object. Then in blocking AST, the client can check this list to find out the conflict outstanding extents as all I/O RPC slots are used out by direct I/O.

Otherwise, If we use original way that match DLM lock, add read-ahead page into queue list and release the matched DLM lock; repeat it for read-ahead and finally submit all I/Os (osc_io_submit), the conflict lockdone extents may be added into the list @oo_reading_exts after the check in blocking AST. And on the client the blocking AST for the server-side locking for DIO will try to lock the pages in these lockdone extents (all pages in lockdone extents are PG_locked, and this extent maybe watis for RPC slots and all RPC slots are used out by DIO). Thus it may cause deadlock.

 

The second patch gives another solution: for each matched read-ahead DLM extent lock, tag the last read-ahead page (osc_page), and increase the tagged accounting for the OSC object. After submit all I/O and add lockdone extents for read-ahead pages into the list @oo_reading_exts, reduce the tagged accounting. Once tagged count becomes zero, wake up waiters. In lock blocing AST, we first wait until the tagged count becomes zero, and then check @oo_reading_exts list to avoid the deadlock.

 

The first patch has passed the customized Maloo test (test_99b).

The second patch has passed in my local testing (I think it can achieve the same effort with solution 1).

 

Could you please review these two solutions, give some advice which one is better?

 

(BTW, test_99a sometimes failed due to PCC mmap problem, I will fix it later.

 

Regards,

Qian

There may be a couple of other ways to fix this issue:

  • don't allow sending lockless DIO RPCs when the client is already holding a DLM lock on that object.
  • similar to the previous option, but have the lockless DIO RPC block on the local DLM lock while it is in-use by another thread and then "pre-cancel" the DLM lock with the RPCs under the blocked lock. This is not great, because it may block the new IO for a long time.
  • have the "lockless DIO" requests still send a DLM lock handle in the OSS RPC, so that the server doesn't try to get another lock. This might make Patrick unhappy, since it will add a DLM lock lookup to every DIO syscall, but it allows the "lockless DIO" and regular BIO to be submitted and processed at the same time.

"Qian Yingjin <qian@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/52711
Subject: LU-17190 osc: force I/O when all RPC slots used out by DIO
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 612a050c6386a18306d627a095e3ad29d1455177
